subject: Databases
title: Transaction Boundaries
description: A quick win: understand Transaction Boundaries.

hook.text: A developer opened a transaction, ran a query, and forgot to commit. The connection held locks for 8 hours.

buildup.text: Transaction boundaries matter: open late, close early. Hold the transaction only for essential operations.

discovery.text: Don't put HTTP calls or file operations inside a transaction. External calls can hang, keeping locks open.

twist.text: ORMs can hide transaction boundaries. A request handler might silently keep a transaction open for the entire request.

climax.text: Set statement_timeout and idle_in_transaction_session_timeout in Postgres to kill runaway transactions.

punchline.text: Open late, close early. Never hold locks waiting on external calls.

quiz.question: Why should you avoid making HTTP calls inside a database transaction?
options: External calls can hang, keeping database locks held for an unpredictable amount of time
HTTP calls automatically roll back transactions
Databases block all network traffic during transactions
